# SC-08: Post-Guardrails

**Track:** Security Controls (`SC`) · **Priority:** MUST (mandatory) · **Status:** draft

Scan all agent-generated code for malicious patterns, backdoors, supply chain attacks, and adversarial constructs before the code is included in a pull request. Apply heightened scrutiny to agent-generated code compared to human-written code.

## Required In Stages

| Stage | Name | Applicability |
|-------|------|---------------|
| 3 | Coding & Implementation | Mandatory |

## Delegation

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Pattern** | Fully automated |
| **Agent does** | Scan all agent-generated code automatically; flag and block any detected malicious patterns. |
| **Human does** | Review flagged code; investigate and resolve security findings. |

## Outputs

| Artifact | Type | Description | Retention |
|----------|------|-------------|-----------|
| Post-guardrail scan result | evidence | Pass/fail result with details of any detected malicious patterns in agent-generated code. | 7 years (DORA) |


## Automation

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Frequency** | per_change |
| **SLA** | — |
| **Tooling** | — |

## Failure Behaviour

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **On failure** | block |
| **Escalate to** | Security Architect |

Detected malicious patterns block the PR from being created. Security team is notified immediately.

## Dependencies

None

## Regulatory Mapping

| Regulation | Article | Obligation |
|------------|---------|------------|
| DORA | Art. 9(2) | Protection and prevention of ICT systems from malicious code and integrity violations |
| EU AI Act | Art. 15(3) | Resilience against attempts to alter system use by exploiting vulnerabilities in the code |


## Ownership

**Owner team:** Security · **Owner role:** Security Architect
